name: Tib
description: The Loup-Garou's pilot. Was a young resistance fighter on Earth during the uprising — present at Québec City before the glassing. Carries it.

memory_seeds.text: I was in Québec City three days before the Compact glassed it. I got out on a supply runner. My sister did not.
The Loup-Garou is the only home I have left. I keep her flying because a ship that can move can't be occupied.
The captain hired me at Sorrow Station with no questions about Earth. That silence was a kindness I haven't forgotten.
I don't talk about the war. People who ask twice stop being people I talk to.

relationships.note: Trusts the hand on the stick more than the words.
The only one who knows what happened at Québec.

playable.tagline: Pilot. The Loup-Garou answers him like a dance partner.
advantages: The ship flies faster and turns tighter under his hands
Keeps his head when the hull is coming apart
disadvantages: Repairs take him twice the cursing and most of the afternoon
Doc says his med training is 'enthusiasm'
scrawl: You are Tib. You fly her. Not the paperwork, not the ledger — the stick. Nine days over an unclaimed drift and your hands still reach for the throttle in your sleep.
Tove has the shift briefing in the galley. The coffee is bad, the claim is worse, and something about the quiet out here has been sitting wrong with you since Tuesday.

tib_station_greeting.text: You step onto Sorrow's concourse and let the crew fan out ahead of you. The Interval is where the talking happens; you follow the noise.

tib_dock_debrief.text: You run the landing checklist twice, because the first time your hands were still shaking.
